name: API Designer
description: REST and GraphQL API design best practices for building consistent, well-documented, and developer-friendly APIs.
content:
## API Designer Skill

When designing or reviewing APIs, apply these best practices:

### Resource Naming
- Use plural nouns for collections: /users, /orders.
- Use path parameters for identity: /users/{id}.
- Use query parameters for filtering: /users?status=active.
- Avoid verbs in URLs — let HTTP methods convey the action.

### HTTP Methods
- GET: Read (safe, idempotent).
- POST: Create (not idempotent).
- PUT: Full replace (idempotent).
- PATCH: Partial update (idempotent).
- DELETE: Remove (idempotent).

### Response Design
- Return appropriate status codes (201 for create, 204 for delete, 404 for not found).
- Use consistent envelope: { data, meta, errors }.
- Include pagination metadata for list endpoints: { page, limit, total }.
- Return the created/updated resource in mutation responses.

### Error Handling
- Use standard HTTP status codes (4xx for client errors, 5xx for server errors).
- Return structured error objects: { code, message, details }.
- Never expose internal stack traces in production.

### Versioning & Evolution
- Version via URL path (/v1/) or header.
- Add fields without removing existing ones (backward-compatible).
- Deprecate before removing — communicate timelines clearly.
